message:
feat(fcp-bootstrap): implement real FROST threshold key generation and signing

Replace the placeholder zero-key ceremony completion with actual
FROST (Flexible Round-Optimized Schnorr Threshold) Ed25519 key
generation and threshold signing, using the frost-ed25519 crate.

ceremony.rs:
- Add frost-ed25519 dependency and integrate FROST dealer-based key
  generation into complete_ceremony_with_rng(), producing real
  Ed25519 group public keys and per-participant key packages
- Add ThresholdKeyMaterial struct storing PublicKeyPackage and
  per-index KeyPackages for post-ceremony signing operations
- Add ThresholdSignatureArtifact capturing participating indices,
  domain-separated transcript hash, and aggregate Ed25519 signature
- Implement sign_with_participants_and_rng() performing full FROST
  two-round signing: round1 commitments, round2 signature shares,
  and aggregate verification against the group public key
- Add owner_verifying_key() to extract the Ed25519 verifying key
  from the completed ceremony's group public key bytes
- Add signing_transcript() for domain-separated blake3 hashing of
  participant set, context, and message
- Track participants across phase transitions via sorted_participants()
- Preserve participant list in CeremonyCheckpoint for resume support
- Add add_shares_with_rng() to accept caller-supplied RNG for
  deterministic test simulations
- Implement custom Debug for ThresholdCeremony to avoid exposing
  key material in logs

workflow.rs:
- Extend workflow integration to exercise threshold signing after
  ceremony completion with verification round-trip tests

lib.rs:
- Re-export new threshold signing types

tests/:
- bootstrap_integration.rs: Update ceremony tests for real key output
- golden_vectors.rs: Add golden vector tests for FROST key generation
  reproducibility and threshold signature verification
- no_mock_integration.rs: Add comprehensive end-to-end tests covering
  full ceremony lifecycle through signing, including multi-participant
  threshold scenarios, signing transcript determinism, and participant
  subset selection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -29,6 +29,7 @@ ciborium.workspace = true
 ed25519-dalek.workspace = true
 x25519-dalek.workspace = true
 blake3.workspace = true
+frost-ed25519 = "2.2"
 hkdf.workspace = true
 sha2.workspace = true
 zeroize = { workspace = true, features = ["derive"] }
